title: podman build: can leave stray images behind
body:
This may be deliberate, or a known difference due to the way `podman build` invokes `buildah bud`. Some layer option flags, maybe.
```console
$ cat foo/Containerfile
FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest AS base
RUN true
FROM base
RUN true
FROM base
$ ./bin/podman build -t foo foo
STEP 1: FROM quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest AS base
STEP 2: RUN true
--> Using cache 71b7cbfa0f82569fa8689cc8a939cc3d1abed84bef03a7ec1449e75d46894679
--> 71b7cbfa0f8
STEP 3: FROM 71b7cbfa0f82569fa8689cc8a939cc3d1abed84bef03a7ec1449e75d46894679
STEP 4: RUN true
--> Using cache b2cc7ec6c5fd3e033caa553d5269a0016fdd1c684e74629750f916aa63b938e7
--> b2cc7ec6c5f
STEP 5: FROM 71b7cbfa0f82569fa8689cc8a939cc3d1abed84bef03a7ec1449e75d46894679
STEP 6: COMMIT foo
--> 71b7cbfa0f8
71b7cbfa0f82569fa8689cc8a939cc3d1abed84bef03a7ec1449e75d46894679
$ ./bin/podman images
REPOSITORY             TAG     IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
<none>                 <none>  b2cc7ec6c5fd  2 minutes ago  5.85 MB       <--- UNWANTED
localhost/foo          latest  71b7cbfa0f82  2 minutes ago  5.85 MB
quay.io/libpod/alpine  latest  961769676411  18 months ago  5.85 MB
```
Compare to `buildah bud -t foo foo`: buildah does not leave that stray image behind.

Why I care: in #9296 I'm trying to run the `buildah bud` test suite using `podman build`; the `bud-multistage-reused` test fails:
```console
# buildah rmi -f foo
untagged: localhost/foo:latest
unable to delete "6832d74fe14697c61a33e449c5f4fc75de3c720a7bc0180030af3a0733daf4a2" (cannot be forced) - image has dependent child images
```
It's perfectly fine to close this with "yeah, expected" or "not a big deal". If so, I'll bypass that test.

comments.body: This sounds like an expected thing (our default use of layer caching vs Buildah's default disabling of it).

Yup 
`podman build --layers=true` by default
`buildah bud --layers=false` by default
